history.historicalImportance: Araria is an important district of the Seemanchal region in northeastern Bihar. The district has long been known for its agricultural economy, cultural diversity and strategic location near the India–Nepal border.
history.formationHistory: Araria was carved out of the erstwhile Purnia district and became an independent district on 1 October 1990 to improve administrative efficiency and regional development.
history.freedomMovement: The people of Araria actively participated in India's freedom movement through the Non-Cooperation Movement, Civil Disobedience Movement and Quit India Movement. Several local freedom fighters contributed to the national struggle.
history.archaeologicalImportance: Although Araria does not have many major archaeological monuments, the district has several ancient temples, historical sites and cultural heritage locations reflecting the history of the Seemanchal region.
historicalTimeline: Ancient period – The region formed part of the ancient kingdoms of Mithila and Anga.
Medieval period – Came under the rule of the Delhi Sultanate and later the Mughal Empire.
1765 – The region came under the administration of the British East India Company.
1947 – Became part of independent India after Independence.
1 October 1990 – Araria became a separate district after being carved out of Purnia district.

geography.location: Araria district is located in the northeastern part of Bihar in the Seemanchal region. It shares an international border with Nepal and is known for its fertile alluvial plains.
geography.climate: Humid subtropical climate with hot summers, heavy monsoon rainfall and cool winters.

specialEconomicFeatures: Major producer of maize and jute

agriculture.dairyDevelopment: Dairy farming is an important source of livelihood in Araria, supported by milk cooperative societies and private dairy collection centres.
agriculture.fisheries: Fish farming is practiced in ponds, oxbow lakes and other inland water bodies across the district.

infrastructure.roadNetwork: Araria district has a developing road network connecting all blocks and major towns through National Highways, State Highways and rural roads.
infrastructure.airConnectivity: The nearest airport is Biratnagar Airport (Nepal). The nearest major Indian airport is Bagdogra Airport, West Bengal.
infrastructure.publicTransport: Bihar State Road Transport buses, private buses, taxis, auto-rickshaws and e-rickshaws provide transport services across the district.
infrastructure.electricity: Most urban and rural areas have electricity connectivity under the Bihar state power distribution network.
infrastructure.waterSupply: Water supply is provided through urban water supply schemes, hand pumps and rural piped water schemes under the Jal Jeevan Mission.
infrastructure.internetConnectivity: 4G mobile networks and broadband internet services are available in most parts of the district, with 5G services expanding in urban areas.

education.governmentSchools: More than 2,500 government schools under the Bihar Education Department.
education.privateSchools: Numerous CBSE, ICSE and Bihar Board affiliated private schools operate across the district.
universities: No university is located in the district. Most colleges are affiliated with Purnea University, Purnea.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district

healthcare.primaryHealthCentres: Primary Health Centres are functional in all blocks under the Bihar Health Department.
medicalColleges: No medical college in the district
healthcare.ambulanceServices: 102 and 108 ambulance services are available throughout the district.

cultureAndHeritage.traditionalDress: Men traditionally wear Dhoti-Kurta or Kurta-Pajama, while women commonly wear Saree and Salwar-Kameez.
historicalHeritage: Ancient temples of the Seemanchal region
culturalOrganizations: District Art and Culture Office, Araria

wildlifeSanctuaries: No wildlife sanctuary within the district

sportsAchievements: District athletes regularly participate in state-level sports competitions.
School and college teams represent Araria in Bihar-level tournaments.

bankingAndFinance.atms: ATMs of major public sector, private sector and regional rural banks are available across Araria district.
bankingAndFinance.digitalBanking: UPI, Internet Banking, Mobile Banking, Aadhaar Enabled Payment System (AEPS) and RuPay services are widely available.
financialInclusionPrograms: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana

governanceAndPublicServices.districtAdministration: The district is administered by the District Magistrate (DM) through various administrative departments and block offices.
governanceAndPublicServices.policeAdministration: Law and order is maintained by Araria District Police under the Bihar Police.
governanceAndPublicServices.fireServices: Fire stations operate in Araria and Forbesganj under the Bihar Fire Services.
governanceAndPublicServices.publicDistributionSystem: Essential commodities are distributed through the Public Distribution System (PDS) and Fair Price Shops across the district.
eGovernanceServices: Bihar Right to Public Services Portal

lawAndOrder.policeDistrict: Araria Police District under Bihar Police
lawAndOrder.trafficPolice: Traffic Police regulates traffic and road safety in Araria town and other major urban areas.
lawAndOrder.disasterManagement: The District Disaster Management Authority (DDMA), Araria coordinates disaster preparedness, flood management, relief and rescue operations.

mediaAndCommunication.postalServices: India Post provides postal, Speed Post, parcel and financial services through head and branch post offices across the district.
mediaAndCommunication.telecommunication: 4G mobile connectivity is available across most of the district, with 5G services gradually expanding in major towns.

stateRankings: Important district of the Seemanchal region
One of Bihar's leading maize-producing districts
nationalRankings: Known for agricultural production, especially maize and jute
Strategically important due to its proximity to the India–Nepal border
notableAchievements: Major producer of maize and jute in Bihar
Significant contribution to Bihar's agricultural economy
developmentIndicators: Expansion of irrigation and flood management projects
records: Araria is one of Bihar's key border districts connecting India with Nepal.

famousPersonalities: Taslima Nasrin (associated with the region during her stay)
importantDates: 1 October 1990 – Araria became a separate district after being carved out of Purnia district.
15 August 1947 – Became part of independent India.
additionalInformation: Araria shares an international border with Nepal.
The district is a major producer of maize and jute in Bihar.
Floods during the monsoon significantly influence the district's economy and daily life.
